run_summary: Screen 1 faces a large basket (10 flags) from two distinct triggers: Google I/O Search redesign (7 flags) and Anthropic Opus 4.8 agent tool (3 flags). Five existing positions show early recovery; most new flags pass BUY eligibility but basket-rule concentration requires exploratory tier.

entries.name: Workiva Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
entries.thesis: Workiva's moat is the auditor-signed-off, XBRL-tagged, SOX-compliant report — AI can draft text but cannot replace the audit trail or regulatory submission workflow. Google Search redesign is entirely orthogonal. The sell-off is generic vertical-SaaS contagion.
entries.what_kills: A Big-4 auditor adopting an LLM-native reporting tool that bypasses Workiva; CSRD-driven demand collapse confirmed in next print (a real, non-AI risk already in the filings).
entries.reasoning: Confidence 4, threat_assessment 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Filings evidence is solid: >35% SEC filings revenue, XBRL/SOX/audit-trail moats. AI mentioned only as generic competitive risk, not as a search-engine threat. Sympathy drop on Google Search redesign is pure thematic contagion unrelated to regulatory-grade auditor workflows. Google Search redesign has zero mechanical pathway to Workiva's audit-trail revenue. 5-day move -4.39%, volume 0.53x = light volume on modest drop, favorable entry. Assigned exploratory tier due to Google Search trigger basket (7 flags) triggering basket rule: explorer-only default to avoid 3+ conviction bets on a single trigger read.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes audit-trail and regulatory-grade output are genuinely irreplaceable, but does not address whether LLM-augmented workflows could reduce Workiva's addressable market if auditors adopt hybrid models that bypass traditional XBRL tagging.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis correctly identifies that Google Search redesign has no mechanical pathway to Workiva's audit workflows. However, the reasoning conflates two separate AI risks: (1) text generation (irrelevant to Workiva) and (2) LLM-native regulatory reporting that bypasses XBRL altogether. The flag itself cites 'A Big-4 auditor adopting an LLM-native reporting tool' as a kill-condition, suggesting this is a real, live risk in the filings—not a theoretical worry. The bull case does not explain why this risk is currently underpriced into the -4.39% five-day move, nor whether the current entry price reflects appropriate skepticism of Big-4 adoption timelines.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
entries.thesis: Google's consumer search agent has no mechanical pathway into nCino's revenue stream — bank loan origination and onboarding sit behind OCC/FDIC oversight, on Salesforce, with multi-year non-cancelable contracts. The sympathy drop on a search-overhaul headline is thematic SaaS-is-doomed reflex, not a re-pricing of any disclosed risk.
entries.what_kills: A bank or large FI publicly piloting an LLM-agent stack to replace nCino workflows; nCino guiding down on renewal economics; a regulator endorsing AI-agent loan origination
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence 4, threat_assessment 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Core business is cloud banking-software embedded in FIs with multi-year non-cancelable Salesforce contracts. Google search agent has no pathway into OCC/FDIC-regulated loan origination workflows. Filings mention AI as generic competitor risk, not search-overhaul risk. Volume 1.36x with only -0.79% price recovery suggests algorithmic selling, not fundamental reset. Part of Google Search basket (7 flags); basket rule applies, exploratory tier enforced.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes OCC/FDIC regulatory moat and Salesforce lock-in are permanent, but does not test whether a regulator-blessed LLM-agent pathway for loan origination could materially compress nCino's serviceable market over 24–36 months.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis correctly notes that Google Search has no pathway into bank loan origination. However, the setup conflates a consumer search redesign with the broader LLM-agent adoption trend in regulated finance. The flag's own kill-condition names 'A bank piloting an LLM-agent stack to replace nCino workflows' as a live risk, implying the market is not fully pricing the probability or timeline of that scenario. At -0.79% five-day performance (actually a gain despite a -5.23% one-day drop), the market signal is mixed and does not clearly support either a panic or a confident buy on entry logic alone.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
entries.thesis: Paycom is the system of record for employee compensation — payroll tax filing, W-2 generation, ACH funds movement. A Google search-agent product does not interact with payroll tax compliance or fund-flow plumbing. The sympathy drop is pure HCM/SaaS reflex on an unrelated trigger.
entries.what_kills: A competitor demonstrating an LLM-agent that handles multi-state payroll tax filing end-to-end; large client losses cited as AI-displacement; embedded-payroll competitors taking share at an accelerating rate (this risk IS in the 10-K and warrants monitoring)
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence 4, threat_assessment 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Core is payroll tax compliance, ACH processing, Vault Visa card—bank-of-record functions requiring regulatory licensing. Google search agent has zero overlap with multi-state payroll tax filing or fund-flow plumbing. Filings note AI as competitive risk and internal investment (LLM on internal network), not as search-substitution threat. Volume 0.65x, 5-day move -4.31%, no fresh catalyst. Pure HCM/SaaS contagion on unrelated trigger. Part of Google Search basket; exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case asserts that payroll tax compliance cannot be automated by LLMs, but does not quantify the risk that embedded payroll competitors (flagged as a real concern in the 10-K) could accelerate share loss if they integrate LLM assistance into multi-state tax filing.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis correctly notes Google Search redesign does not address payroll tax compliance. However, the flag itself lists 'embedded-payroll competitors taking share at an accelerating rate' as an actual risk 'in the 10-K and warrants monitoring.' The bull reasoning dismisses this entirely and leans instead on the unrelated Google trigger. While the 5-day move of -4.31% on 0.65x volume is light, the lack of explicit guidance about embedded-payroll competitive pressure in the bull case is a gap; the critique would be stronger if it acknowledged this known risk and explained why current valuation already prices it.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
entries.thesis: Verra's revenue depends on municipal contracts (NYCDOT alone is ~18% of revenue, just renewed for 5 years), physical camera networks, and statutory authorization for automated enforcement. None of these are touched by a Google consumer search redesign. The drop reads as 'IT Services / SaaS-tagged' basket selling.
entries.what_kills: Additional jurisdictions following Ontario in banning automated speed enforcement; loss of one of the three large Commercial Services customers currently in renewal (this risk is now flagged in the 10-Q and is the real Verra-specific story)
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence 4, threat_assessment 'none', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Business: automated photo enforcement and tolling on multi-year municipal contracts with physical camera hardware and court-admissible evidence chains. Google Search redesign is entirely orthogonal. Filings list AI as operational risk Verra itself uses, not as substitution threat. NYCDOT contract just renewed for 5 years (~18% revenue). Volume 1.42x on modest -2.17% 5-day move indicates light selling. Real Verra risk (Ontario speed-enforcement ban) is separate from trigger. Part of Google Search basket; exploratory tier enforced.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case asserts that physical camera networks and statutory authorization are moats, but does not address the weight of the Ontario speed-enforcement ban or quantify how many additional U.S. jurisdictions are reviewing similar bans—a risk explicitly flagged in the 10-Q.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis correctly notes that Google Search redesign is orthogonal to municipal traffic-enforcement contracts. However, the flag's own kill-condition names 'Additional jurisdictions following Ontario in banning automated speed enforcement' as a live risk 'now flagged in the 10-Q and is the real Verra-specific story.' The bull reasoning dismisses this entirely and attributes the -2.17% five-day drop purely to basket contagion. The real question is whether Ontario's ban is already priced into the current -3.04% one-day move; if it is not, this is a material gap in the bull's analysis, not a vindication of the sympathy-fade thesis.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
entries.thesis: Photronics has zero business-model overlap with Anthropic's agent-orchestration capability. The magnitude of today's drop is far beyond a sympathy fade and suggests company-specific news (possibly earnings/guidance — 8x volume, -36%). Cannot confidently classify as pure sympathy-fade without confirming no negative catalyst.
entries.what_kills: An earnings miss, lost-customer 8-K, or impairment charge today would invalidate the sympathy-fade thesis entirely — this move is too large to be pure trigger sympathy.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 2 is below min_buy_confidence (3). Additionally, move_pct -36.42 and volume_multiple 8.38 are too large to confidently classify as sympathy fade—flag author correctly notes 'Cannot confidently classify as pure sympathy-fade without confirming no negative catalyst.' The threshold for a sympathy-fade entry on this screen requires confidence that the panic is *unjustified*, not merely mysterious.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
entries.thesis: AEIS provides power delivery for fabs and data centers — both of which expand with AI buildout. The 10-K explicitly names AI as a demand driver. A drop on an Anthropic agent-tool release is mechanically backwards: more AI agents running means more inference compute means more data center power conditioning demand.
entries.what_kills: Data center capex slowdown signal from a hyperscaler; major customer loss; semi-cycle downturn.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (days_held 2, unrealized -11.03%). Screen 1 does not support ADD; position decision issued above (HOLD).

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
entries.thesis: DOCN is a developer-focused IaaS/PaaS provider. A Google Search overhaul has no meaningful pathway to harm DOCN's revenue — developers don't discover droplets via blue-link SEO. The sell-off is pure thematic 'AI bad for cloud SaaS' contagion, contradicted by the filings showing AI/ML as an embraced product line.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that DOCN's marketplace or community-traffic acquisition is materially Google-search-dependent; or a hyperscaler price-war announcement that genuinely compresses cloud SMB margins.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (days_held 2, unrealized -1.81%). Position decision issued above (HOLD).

entries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
entries.thesis: Tenable is a continuous-scanning vulnerability-management platform with agent footprint across hybrid infrastructure. The Google Search overhaul cannot touch any part of its revenue model. The filings actively argue AI-enhanced attacks INCREASE demand for their product. The sell-off is generic 'AI is bad for security software' contagion.
entries.what_kills: Evidence a hyperscaler-bundled AI security tool is taking share at enterprise level; or weak Q2 print on its own merits.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (days_held 2, unrealized +11.22%). Position decision issued above (HOLD).

entries.name: Qualys, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
entries.thesis: Qualys runs continuous scanning and compliance-grade reporting (PCI, HIPAA, audit trail) — none of which Google Search displaces. Filings show AI integrated into the product and AI-driven threats as a tailwind to demand. Sympathy drop is pure sector contagion.
entries.what_kills: A major customer publicly migrating off Qualys to a hyperscaler-native scanner; weak guide on its own merit.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (days_held 2, unrealized +8.98%). Position decision issued above (HOLD).

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
entries.thesis: Onto sells process-control/metrology equipment essential for advanced-node fabs. The Anthropic agent-orchestration release has no mechanical pathway to reduce demand for semiconductor inspection equipment — if anything, AI accelerator complexity increases inspection intensity. A modest drop on an Anthropic-day is a classic mistaken sympathy fade.
entries.what_kills: Genuine semi-cap cycle inflection (memory or logic capex cut), or a customer-concentration shock at a top buyer.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (days_held 0, unrealized -3.14%). Position decision issued above (HOLD).

entries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
entries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
entries.thesis: Evolent has zero search-overhaul exposure, so the AI-trigger sympathy story is unjustified on the trigger. BUT the stock has been weak for fundamental reasons (oncology cost overruns, Medicare/Medicaid policy headwinds, $3.83 price tape suggests distressed), and today's move could easily be continued idiosyncratic selling unrelated to the trigger. Calling this an 'unjustified panic buy' on a $3.83 stock with real ongoing risk-corridor problems is not the trade this screen is built for.
entries.reasoning: Classification is 'UNCLEAR', not 'OVERDONE'. Confidence 2 is below min_buy_confidence (3). Additionally, panic_calibration is 'partial', not 'unjustified'. Flag author correctly notes the stock has real ongoing risks (oncology cost overruns, Medicare/Medicaid policy, Molina 25.7% concentration, risk-corridor exposure) unrelated to search redesign, making it hard to isolate sympathy fade from idiosyncratic weakness. Does not meet Screen 1 BUY eligibility.
